name: Laukahi_ The Hawaiʻi Plant Conservation Network

properties.OrgName: Laukahi: The Hawaiʻi Plant Conservation Network
properties.ScialMedia: FB - Laukahi: Hawaii Plant Conservation Network, IG - laukahi_network_hawaii
properties.Mission: Laukahi: The Hawaiʻi Plant Conservation Network, is a self-directed, voluntary alliance of agencies, organizations, and individuals working with a common goal to protect Hawaiʻi's rare and threatened native plant species and their habitats through coordinated conservation efforts, fulfilling the objectives of the Hawaiʻi Strategy for Plant Conservation. Laukahi serves to enhance and expand plant conservation in Hawaiʻi by uniting our collective efforts, aligning our shared goals towards a more comprehensive approach, and promoting awareness of Hawaiʻi's significant contribution to global biodiversity conservation.
properties.Metrics: We track our partner organizations' activities: acres fenced (polygons), number of Species of Conservation Importance (SCI) outplanted/conserved/preserved in situ, % ecoregions preserved, number of research agenda topics published, number of SCI in ex situ facilities, number of duplicate ex situ collections, number of conservation plans (for SCI) developed, number of biosecurity measures in place and where, acres of land (polygons) where high plant biodiversity exists protected and not, number of people working in conservation field, capacity of ex situ facilities, and number of partnerships that exist at the local, state, national, and international levels.
properties.Goals: The primary outcome of our efforts will be preventing the extinction of any more native Hawaiian plants. We engage and include all who would like to be a part of theses efforts. The more organizations and individuals that we can collaborate with, the more efficient and sustainable our coordinated efforts become. We are all stakeholders in our ecosystems, and we become better stewards when we cooperate.
